assertions.description: navigator.deviceMemory returns a valid power-of-two or fraction bucket value (0.25, 0.5, 1, 2, 4, 8, 16, 32).
The memory-inspector echo endpoint opts in to Sec-CH-Device-Memory and varies on that request header.
assertions.test: (async () => { const response = await fetch('/v147/update-device-memory-api-limits/memory-inspector/client-hint-echo?conformance=opt-in', { cache: 'no-store' }); const json = await response.json(); const acceptCH = response.headers.get('accept-ch') || ''; const vary = response.headers.get('vary') || ''; return response.ok && json.expectedRequestHeader === 'Sec-CH-Device-Memory' && acceptCH.toLowerCase().split(',').map((part) => part.trim()).includes('sec-ch-device-memory') && vary.toLowerCase().includes('sec-ch-device-memory'); })()

assertions.description: The echo endpoint reports the actual Sec-CH-Device-Memory request header as a nullable value rather than simulating support.
assertions.test: (async () => { const response = await fetch('/v147/update-device-memory-api-limits/memory-inspector/client-hint-echo?conformance=echo-shape', { cache: 'no-store' }); const json = await response.json(); const received = json.received?.secCHDeviceMemory; return response.ok && json.received && Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call(json.received, 'secCHDeviceMemory') && (received === null || /^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?$/.test(received)); })()
